name: Photopea
tagline: A full Photoshop-class image editor that runs entirely in your browser.
description: Photopea opens PSD, XCF, Sketch, and RAW files and offers layers, masks, smart objects, and filters — no download, no account. It is one of the strongest demonstrations of what a PWA can do, matching desktop software feature-for-feature.

name: Spotify Web Player
tagline: Stream Spotify's full music and podcast catalog without installing the desktop app.
description: The Spotify web player is an installable PWA with the full streaming catalog, playlists, and podcast support. It launches in its own window and integrates with system media keys.

name: Excalidraw
tagline: A hand-drawn-style virtual whiteboard for sketching diagrams and ideas.
description: Excalidraw is an open-source whiteboard with a distinctive sketchy aesthetic. It works fully offline, requires no account, supports real-time collaboration via shareable links, and exports to PNG and SVG.

name: YouTube Music
tagline: Google's music streaming service, installable straight from the browser.
description: YouTube Music runs as a first-class PWA with background playback in its own window, media-key support, and the full YouTube music catalog including live performances and remixes.

name: Pinterest
tagline: Visual discovery and bookmarking — one of the original PWA success stories.
description: Pinterest's PWA rebuild famously increased engagement by 60% and became a canonical case study. It offers the full pin, board, and discovery experience with push notifications and a fast, app-like feel.

name: Telegram Web
tagline: Full-featured Telegram messaging from any browser.
description: Telegram Web is a complete messaging client with chats, channels, media sharing, and push notifications. It syncs instantly with your phone and installs as a standalone app window.

name: VS Code for the Web
tagline: Visual Studio Code running fully in the browser, no install required.
description: vscode.dev brings the real VS Code editor to the browser: open local folders with the File System Access API, edit GitHub repos directly, and use many extensions. Installs as a desktop-class PWA.

name: Squoosh
tagline: Compress and convert images with instant side-by-side comparison.
description: Squoosh, built by the Chrome team, compresses images to WebP, AVIF, MozJPEG and more — entirely on-device, so nothing is uploaded. It works fully offline and weighs almost nothing.

name: Todoist
tagline: A polished cross-platform task manager with offline support.
description: Todoist's web app is an installable PWA with natural-language task entry, projects, labels, and reminders. Changes made offline sync automatically when you reconnect.

name: StackBlitz
tagline: Instant full-stack dev environments that boot in your browser.
description: StackBlitz runs Node.js inside the browser via WebContainers, so npm installs and dev servers work with no local setup. Great for prototyping, bug repros, and sharing runnable examples.

name: Uber
tagline: Request rides from a web app small enough to load on 2G.
description: Uber built m.uber.com so riders on low-end devices and slow networks could book without the native app. The core ride-request flow loads in seconds even on constrained connections.

name: Starbucks
tagline: Browse the menu and order ahead — even while offline.
description: The Starbucks PWA lets customers browse the menu, customize drinks, and build orders offline, syncing when a connection returns. It is roughly 99% smaller than the iOS app and doubled daily active web users.

name: Monkeytype
tagline: A minimalist, endlessly customizable typing test.
description: Monkeytype is the most popular typing practice site on the web, with themes, test modes, and detailed stats. It installs as a lightweight PWA and needs no account to use.

name: Proxx
tagline: A space-themed Minesweeper that runs on anything, even feature phones.
description: Proxx, built by the Chrome team as a PWA showcase, is a polished Minesweeper-style game that works offline and runs smoothly on everything from flagship phones to KaiOS feature phones.

name: YouTube
tagline: The world's largest video platform, installable as an app.
description: YouTube's site is a full PWA: install it for an app-like window with the complete video catalog, subscriptions, and playlists. Works with system media keys and picture-in-picture.

name: X (Twitter)
tagline: The full X/Twitter experience — the original 'Twitter Lite' PWA.
description: X's web app descends from Twitter Lite, one of the most influential PWAs ever shipped. Timeline, posting, DMs, and push notifications, in a fraction of the native app's size.

name: TikTok
tagline: Browse and post TikTok videos from an installable web app.
description: TikTok's website is an installable PWA with the full For You feed, search, and posting. Handy for watching without the native app's battery and data appetite.

name: GitHub
tagline: The world's largest code-hosting platform, installable from the browser.
description: GitHub ships a web app manifest, so you can install it and manage repositories, issues, and pull requests in a standalone window. Pairs well with vscode.dev for editing.

name: Duolingo
tagline: Gamified language lessons in an installable web app.
description: Duolingo's web app covers the full course catalog with streaks, leagues, and practice reminders. Installing the PWA gives you lessons in their own window without the mobile app.

name: SoundCloud
tagline: Stream independent music and podcasts from an installable player.
description: SoundCloud's web player installs as a PWA with the platform's entire catalog of independent artists, DJ sets, and podcasts, plus system media-key support.

name: Chess.com
tagline: Play chess online with millions of players, puzzles, and lessons.
description: Chess.com's web app is installable and offers live games, daily correspondence chess, tactics puzzles, and lessons — the complete experience without the native app.

name: Lichess
tagline: Free, open-source, ad-free online chess.
description: Lichess is a beloved open-source chess server: instant games, puzzles, studies, and tournaments with no ads and no account required. Fast, lightweight, and installable.

name: Replit
tagline: Write, run, and host code in dozens of languages from the browser.
description: Replit is a full development environment in a tab: editors, terminals, package management, and one-click hosting. Installing the PWA makes it feel like a desktop IDE.

name: diagrams.net (draw.io)
tagline: Free flowchart and diagram editor that works fully offline.
description: diagrams.net (formerly draw.io) is the standard free tool for flowcharts, network diagrams, and org charts. The PWA works completely offline and saves to local files or cloud drives without an account.

name: The Weather Channel
tagline: Forecasts, radar, and severe-weather alerts on the web.
description: weather.com is an installable PWA with local forecasts, interactive radar maps, and severe-weather alerts — one of the early large-scale PWA deployments.

name: Mastodon
tagline: Decentralized, open-source social network you can install.
description: Mastodon's web client is a PWA on every server; mastodon.social is the flagship instance run by the Mastodon nonprofit. Timelines, posting, and push notifications, no algorithmic feed.

name: Windy
tagline: Stunning animated wind, weather, and radar maps.
description: Windy visualizes wind, rain, temperature, and dozens of other layers on a beautiful animated globe. Loved by pilots, sailors, and storm watchers; installs as a PWA.

name: tldraw
tagline: A fast, minimal infinite-canvas whiteboard.
description: tldraw is an open-source infinite canvas for quick sketches and diagrams with real-time multiplayer. Instant to load, no account needed, installable as an app.

name: Pixlr
tagline: Quick photo editing and templates in the browser.
description: Pixlr offers layer-based photo editing and design templates aimed at fast social-media work. Lighter than Photopea for quick fixes, and installable as a PWA.

tagline: The original tile-sliding puzzle game by its creator.
description: Gabriele Cirulli's original 2048 — slide tiles, merge numbers, chase the 2048 tile. The official site is a tiny installable PWA, perfect for offline commutes.

name: Financial Times
tagline: The FT's pioneering offline-capable news web app.
description: app.ft.com is one of the earliest serious news PWAs: the Financial Times built it to escape app stores, with offline reading and fast load on any device. Subscription required for full access.

name: PWABuilder
tagline: Microsoft's tool for packaging PWAs for app stores.
description: PWABuilder analyzes your PWA, scores its manifest and service worker, and packages it for the Microsoft, Google, and Apple stores. Fittingly, it is itself an installable PWA.

name: Gmail
tagline: Google's email service, installable with offline support.
description: Gmail installs as a PWA with search, labels, and offline access to recent mail. Our automated verification can't see past its sign-in page, so it carries the at-your-own-risk label — check that the address is mail.google.com before signing in.

name: Instagram
tagline: Instagram's feed, stories, and DMs from an installable web app.
description: Instagram's website is installable and covers the feed, stories, search, and messaging. It loads its app manifest dynamically, which our automated verification can't observe.

name: WhatsApp Web
tagline: WhatsApp messaging paired to your phone, in the browser.
description: WhatsApp Web mirrors your phone's chats with full messaging, media, and calls in supported browsers. It refuses automated requests, so it can't be machine-verified — pair only at web.whatsapp.com.

name: Canva
tagline: Drag-and-drop design for presentations, posters, and social posts.
description: Canva's editor installs as a PWA with thousands of templates for social graphics, slides, and print. It blocks automated checks, so it carries the at-your-own-risk label.

name: Google Photos
tagline: Photo backup, search, and editing in an installable web app.
description: Google Photos works as a PWA for browsing, searching, and lightly editing your photo library. Automated verification can't see past its sign-in page.

name: Outlook
tagline: Microsoft's email and calendar as an installable web app.
description: Outlook on the web installs as a PWA with mail, calendar, and contacts. Like Gmail, it hides everything behind sign-in, so automated verification can't inspect it.

name: Google Meet
tagline: Video meetings straight from the browser, no download.
description: Google Meet runs entirely on the web and installs as a PWA for quick access to calls. Automated verification can't see past its account gate.

name: Bluesky
tagline: The open-protocol social network, installable from bsky.app.
description: Bluesky's web client is installable and covers the full experience: feeds, posting, and custom algorithms on the AT Protocol. Its manifest loads via scripts our checker can't run.

name: Flipkart
tagline: India's biggest online store — home of the famous Flipkart Lite PWA.
description: Flipkart Lite was the case study that put PWAs on the map for e-commerce. The current site no longer exposes its manifest to automated tools, so it carries the at-your-own-risk label.

name: DEV Community
tagline: A friendly blogging community for software developers.
description: dev.to was long a showcase open-source PWA known for instant page loads. Its current pages don't expose a manifest to our checker, so it's listed unverified.

name: Pocket Casts
tagline: A polished podcast player for the web.
description: Pocket Casts' web player syncs subscriptions and playback position across devices. The landing page doesn't expose the manifest to automated checks, so it's listed unverified.

name: TIDAL
tagline: Hi-fi music streaming in the browser.
description: TIDAL's web player streams its lossless catalog and installs for app-like listening. It blocks automated requests, so it can't be machine-verified.

name: BitMidi
tagline: Listen to a huge archive of free MIDI files.
description: Listen to a huge archive of free MIDI files. Community-sourced listing (found via the awesome-pwa list on GitHub); Web App Finder has not verified the publisher — use at your own risk.

name: Joybox
tagline: A pinboard for audiovisual media.
description: A pinboard for audiovisual media. Community-sourced listing (found via the awesome-pwa list on GitHub); Web App Finder has not verified the publisher — use at your own risk.

description: Bangle.io is an open-source note app that edits Markdown files directly on your device using the browser's file-system access — your notes never leave your computer.

name: Taskade
tagline: Outlines, mind maps, and task lists for teams in one workspace.
description: Taskade blends to-do lists, structured outlines, and mind maps with real-time collaboration and built-in video chat, syncing across every device.

name: Taiga
tagline: Open-source agile project management with scrum and kanban.
description: Taiga is a long-running open-source project management platform for agile teams, offering backlogs, sprints, kanban boards, and issue tracking.

name: Hyperdraft
tagline: Turn your notes into a lightweight personal website.
description: Hyperdraft is a minimalist note-taking tool that doubles as a website generator — write notes and publish them instantly. Works offline and requires no account.

name: Notesnook
tagline: Private, end-to-end encrypted note-taking that syncs everywhere.
description: Notesnook is an open-source notes app with zero-knowledge encryption — notebooks, tags, and rich-text notes sync across devices without the company being able to read them.

name: Mermaid Live Editor
tagline: Write text, get flowcharts and diagrams instantly.
description: The official live editor for Mermaid, the text-to-diagram language used across GitHub and much of the software world. Type a description and watch flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and Gantt charts render live.

name: Tana
tagline: An AI-native workspace built on connected outline notes.
description: Tana organizes notes as a graph of connected outline nodes with 'supertags' that turn plain notes into structured data, plus built-in AI features.

name: Linear
tagline: The fast, keyboard-driven issue tracker loved by software teams.
description: Linear is a polished project and issue tracking tool known for its speed and keyboard-first design. Its web app installs cleanly as a PWA on the desktop.

name: Cal.com
tagline: Open-source scheduling — share a link, get booked.
description: Cal.com is the open-source alternative to Calendly: share your booking link and let people schedule meetings that respect your availability across calendars.

name: NocoDB
tagline: Turn any database into a smart spreadsheet interface.
description: NocoDB is a popular open-source Airtable alternative that gives databases a friendly spreadsheet, gallery, and form interface — self-hostable or cloud.

name: AFFiNE
tagline: Docs, whiteboards, and databases merged into one canvas.
description: AFFiNE is an open-source workspace where a page can be a document, an edgeless whiteboard, or a database — a privacy-first alternative to Notion and Miro.

name: Anytype
tagline: A local-first, encrypted 'everything app' for notes and knowledge.
description: Anytype stores your notes, tasks, and collections locally with encryption and peer-to-peer sync — an offline-first take on the personal knowledge base.

name: n8n
tagline: Connect apps and automate workflows with a visual editor.
description: n8n is a leading open-source workflow automation tool — drag nodes onto a canvas to connect hundreds of services, with the option to self-host everything.

name: Baserow
tagline: Open-source no-code database and Airtable alternative.
description: Baserow lets you build databases as easily as spreadsheets, with views, forms, and an API — open source and self-hostable.

name: Element
tagline: Secure, decentralized team chat on the Matrix network.
description: Element is the flagship client for Matrix, the open, decentralized messaging protocol. End-to-end encrypted chats, rooms, and calls — no single company controls the network.

name: Pixelfed
tagline: Ad-free photo sharing on the open fediverse.
description: Pixelfed is a decentralized, open-source photo sharing network — an Instagram alternative with no ads and no tracking, connected to the wider fediverse.

name: Jitsi Meet
tagline: Free video meetings — no account, just share a link.
description: Jitsi Meet is the well-known open-source video conferencing service: start an encrypted meeting instantly and invite anyone with a link, no sign-up required.

name: Nostter
tagline: A web client for the Nostr social protocol.
description: Nostter is a community-built client for Nostr, the decentralized social protocol. Listed from a community directory; we could not fully verify the publisher.

name: Threema Web
tagline: Use the privacy-focused Threema messenger from your browser.
description: Threema Web mirrors the Swiss end-to-end encrypted messenger to your desktop browser — Threema is one of the most privacy-focused paid messengers available.

name: Proton Mail
tagline: Encrypted email from the team behind Proton.
description: Proton Mail is the widely used end-to-end encrypted email service from Switzerland. The web app installs as a PWA and covers mail, contacts, and calendar.

name: HEY
tagline: Opinionated email that screens who can reach you.
description: HEY, from the makers of Basecamp, rethinks email with a screener for first-time senders, a focused Imbox, and built-in privacy protection against tracking pixels.

name: Fastmail
tagline: Fast, professional email with calendars and contacts.
description: Fastmail is a long-established independent email provider. Its web app is famously quick, works as an installable PWA, and includes calendar and contacts.

name: Lofi.co
tagline: Chill lo-fi music with customizable ambient scenes.
description: Lofi.co plays curated lo-fi streams over animated scenes you can customize with rain, café noise, and other ambient sounds — a focus companion for work or study.

name: Wavepad
tagline: A touch-friendly synthesizer you play in the browser.
description: An open-source experimental synth pad — drag your finger or pointer to bend frequency and filters. Simple, free, and works offline.

name: XSound
tagline: A web audio playground for synthesis and effects.
description: XSound is an open-source web audio workstation for experimenting with synthesizers, effects, and recording. Listed from a community directory; we could not fully verify the publisher.

name: AudioMass
tagline: A full audio editor that runs entirely in your browser.
description: AudioMass is an open-source waveform editor — trim, fade, apply effects, and export audio without uploading anything; processing happens on your device.

name: Bandcamp
tagline: Discover and directly support independent musicians.
description: Bandcamp is the leading storefront for independent music — stream albums for free and buy downloads or merch where most of the money goes to the artists.

name: Ambient Mixer
tagline: Mix your own background soundscapes from thousands of channels.
description: Ambient Mixer lets you blend rain, fireplaces, fantasy taverns, and thousands of other community-made audio atmospheres, with individual volume control per sound.

name: Radio Garden
tagline: Spin the globe and tune into live radio anywhere on Earth.
description: Radio Garden puts thousands of live radio stations on a 3D globe — drag to any city in the world and listen to its local stations instantly, no account needed.

name: Poolsuite FM
tagline: Summer-forever internet radio with a retro Mac interface.
description: Poolsuite (formerly Poolside FM) streams sun-soaked disco and pop through a delightfully retro 1980s desktop interface.

name: OpenCut
tagline: A free, open-source video editor in the browser.
description: OpenCut is an open-source CapCut alternative — timeline video editing that runs in the browser with your footage staying on your device.

name: Clideo
tagline: Quick online tools to trim, merge, and compress video.
description: Clideo bundles simple one-task video tools — cut, crop, add subtitles, compress — that work from any browser without installing an editor.

name: VEED
tagline: Online video editing with auto-subtitles and templates.
description: VEED is a popular browser-based video editor aimed at creators and marketers — trim, caption, add music, and export without desktop software.

name: Kapwing
tagline: Collaborative video editing and meme-making online.
description: Kapwing is a well-known collaborative video editor in the browser, with subtitling, resizing, and template tools used widely by social media teams.

name: Descript
tagline: Edit video and podcasts by editing the transcript.
description: Descript transcribes your recording and lets you edit the media by editing text — delete a sentence from the transcript and it's cut from the audio and video.

name: SVGOMG
tagline: Optimize SVG files with a live before/after preview.
description: SVGOMG is the go-to web interface for SVGO, the standard SVG optimizer — drop in an SVG, toggle optimizations, and watch the file size shrink. Works offline.

name: CodeSandbox
tagline: Spin up full development environments in the browser.
description: CodeSandbox runs instant cloud development environments for web projects — prototype, share, and collaborate on code without any local setup.

name: Transform
tagline: Convert between dozens of code and data formats.
description: Transform is a polyglot converter for developers — JSON to TypeScript, SVG to JSX, CSS to Tailwind, and dozens more transformations in one page.

name: CodeImage
tagline: Turn code snippets into beautiful shareable images.
description: CodeImage styles your code snippets with themes, window frames, and backgrounds for sharing on social media or documentation. Open source.

name: JSON Crack
tagline: Visualize JSON, YAML, and CSV as interactive graphs.
description: JSON Crack turns raw data files into zoomable node graphs so you can actually see the structure — a widely used open-source tool for debugging payloads.

name: grep.app
tagline: Search code across a million public GitHub repositories.
description: grep.app searches over a million public GitHub repositories with regex support — a fast way to find real-world usage examples of any API or pattern.

name: Webhook.site
tagline: Instantly inspect any webhook or HTTP request.
description: Webhook.site gives you a unique URL that captures and displays every request sent to it — the standard quick tool for debugging webhooks and callbacks.

name: JWT.io
tagline: Decode and verify JSON Web Tokens on the spot.
description: JWT.io, maintained by Auth0, decodes JSON Web Tokens so you can inspect headers, payloads, and signatures — a staple of every web developer's toolbox.

name: Crontab Guru
tagline: Cron schedule expressions, translated into plain English.
description: Crontab Guru explains cron expressions in human language as you type — the reference site virtually every developer uses to double-check schedules.

name: QuickRef.ME
tagline: Cheat sheets for programming languages and tools.
description: QuickRef.ME collects concise cheat sheets for dozens of languages, frameworks, and Linux commands in one searchable place. Open source.

name: ExplainShell
tagline: Paste a shell command and see every part explained.
description: ExplainShell matches each flag and argument of a shell command against the actual man pages and annotates the whole line — invaluable for decoding cryptic one-liners.

name: Carbon
tagline: Create beautiful images of your source code.
description: Carbon is the widely used open-source tool for turning code snippets into styled, syntax-highlighted images ready for slides and social posts.

name: CodePen
tagline: The front-end playground for HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
description: CodePen is the best-known social development environment for front-end code — build demos live in the browser and explore millions of community 'pens'.

name: PostHog
tagline: Open-source product analytics, session replay, and flags.
description: PostHog bundles product analytics, session recordings, feature flags, and A/B testing in one open-source platform that you can self-host or use in the cloud.

name: Wakapi
tagline: Open-source coding-time tracking, compatible with WakaTime.
description: Wakapi tracks how much time you spend coding per project and language, as a lightweight open-source alternative to WakaTime. Listed from a community directory.

name: Netlify Drop
tagline: Drag a folder onto the page — get a live website.
description: Netlify Drop deploys a static site by literally dragging a folder into the browser window. From Netlify, one of the largest web hosting platforms.

name: Penpot
tagline: Open-source design and prototyping — the Figma alternative.
description: Penpot is the leading open-source design platform for UI design and prototyping, built on web standards (SVG) and free for teams of any size.

name: Graphite
tagline: A free procedural 2D graphics editor in the browser.
description: Graphite is an ambitious open-source 2D graphics editor combining vector drawing with a non-destructive, node-based procedural workflow.

name: CSS Gradient
tagline: Design CSS gradients visually and copy the code.
description: A focused tool for building linear and radial CSS gradients with a visual editor, then copying clean CSS straight into your project.

name: Coolors
tagline: Generate perfect color palettes with the space bar.
description: Coolors is the super-fast palette generator used by millions of designers — tap the space bar to cycle schemes, lock colors you like, and export anywhere.

name: Shots
tagline: Wrap screenshots in gorgeous device mockups.
description: Shots turns plain screenshots into polished presentation images with device frames, backgrounds, and shadows — no design skills needed.

name: remove.bg
tagline: Remove image backgrounds automatically in seconds.
description: remove.bg erases the background from any photo with one click using AI — the best-known dedicated background removal service on the web.

name: Haikei
tagline: Generate unique SVG backgrounds, blobs, and waves.
description: Haikei generates SVG design assets — layered waves, blobs, grids, and gradients — that you can customize and export for websites and slides.

name: Fontjoy
tagline: Generate font pairings with machine learning.
description: Fontjoy suggests Google Font combinations that look good together — lock the fonts you like and regenerate the rest until the pairing feels right.

name: Typescale
tagline: Preview harmonious typographic scales for the web.
description: Typescale previews modular type scales — pick a ratio and instantly see heading and body sizes that work together, with CSS ready to copy.

name: Whimsical
tagline: Flowcharts, wireframes, and mind maps at speed.
description: Whimsical is a fast visual workspace for flowcharts, wireframes, sticky notes, and mind maps, popular with product teams for quick thinking-out-loud diagrams.

name: Lospec
tagline: Pixel art tools, palettes, and tutorials for low-spec art.
description: Lospec is the home of the pixel art community — a huge palette database, browser-based pixel editors, and tutorials for restrictive 'low-spec' art styles.

name: Figma
tagline: The collaborative interface design standard, in your browser.
description: Figma is the industry-standard collaborative design tool — UI design, prototyping, and whiteboarding (FigJam) that runs entirely on the web and installs as an app.

name: DocuSeal
tagline: Open-source document signing — a DocuSign alternative.
description: DocuSeal handles legally binding e-signatures with an open-source core: create fillable PDF forms, send for signature, and self-host if you want full control.

name: Invoice Ninja
tagline: Invoicing, payments, and expenses for freelancers.
description: Invoice Ninja is a long-running open-source invoicing platform — create branded invoices, accept online payments, and track time and expenses.

name: XE Currency
tagline: Live exchange rates and currency conversion since 1993.
description: XE is one of the oldest and most trusted currency sites — live mid-market exchange rates, historical charts, and conversion for every world currency.

name: Contrax
tagline: Simple contract creation and management.
description: A lightweight tool for drafting and managing contracts. Listed from a community directory; we could not fully verify the publisher.

name: Plausible Analytics
tagline: Privacy-first website analytics without cookies.
description: Plausible is the popular lightweight, open-source alternative to Google Analytics — no cookies, no personal data collection, and a simple one-page dashboard.

name: Papermark
tagline: Share documents with link analytics — open-source DocSend.
description: Papermark lets you share pitch decks and documents via trackable links with page-by-page analytics, as an open-source DocSend alternative.

name: Metabase
tagline: Ask questions of your data — open-source BI dashboards.
description: Metabase is one of the most-deployed open-source business intelligence tools: connect a database and build charts and dashboards without writing SQL.

name: Umami
tagline: Simple, privacy-focused web analytics you can self-host.
description: Umami is a popular open-source analytics platform that collects no personal data and needs no cookie banner — a clean dashboard for the metrics that matter.

name: Kommit
tagline: Minimalist spaced-repetition flashcards.
description: Kommit is a small open-source flashcard app using spaced repetition to help memories stick. Works offline with no account.

name: LanguageTool
tagline: Grammar and style checking in over 25 languages.
description: LanguageTool is the leading open-source grammar checker — paste text and get corrections for grammar, punctuation, and style in dozens of languages.

name: Khan Academy
tagline: Free world-class courses from math to history.
description: Khan Academy is the nonprofit education platform offering free lessons, exercises, and mastery tracking across math, science, computing, humanities, and test prep.

name: Brilliant
tagline: Learn math, logic, and CS through interactive puzzles.
description: Brilliant teaches STEM subjects through hands-on interactive problem solving rather than lectures — short guided lessons in math, logic, data, and programming.

name: AnkiWeb
tagline: The web home of Anki spaced-repetition flashcards.
description: AnkiWeb is the official sync service and web client for Anki, the most widely used spaced-repetition flashcard system — study your decks from any browser.

name: Desmos
tagline: The graphing calculator used in classrooms worldwide.
description: Desmos is the beloved free graphing calculator — plot functions, sliders, and tables with instant feedback. Used by millions of students and standardized tests.

name: GeoGebra
tagline: Interactive geometry, algebra, and calculus tools.
description: GeoGebra provides free interactive math apps — dynamic geometry, graphing, 3D, and CAS — used in schools around the world, with a huge library of shared activities.

name: Wolfram Alpha
tagline: Compute expert-level answers to almost anything.
description: Wolfram Alpha is the computational knowledge engine — type a math problem, unit conversion, or factual query and get computed, step-by-step answers.

name: Scrimba
tagline: Interactive coding screencasts you can edit and run.
description: Scrimba teaches web development through 'scrims' — video lessons where you can pause at any moment and edit the instructor's actual code right in the player.

name: Flexbox Froggy
tagline: Learn CSS flexbox by guiding frogs to lily pads.
description: Flexbox Froggy is the classic game for learning CSS flexbox — write real CSS to move frogs onto their lily pads across 24 progressively trickier levels.

name: Overleaf
tagline: Collaborative LaTeX editing for papers and theses.
description: Overleaf is the standard online LaTeX editor — write scientific documents with live preview and real-time collaboration, with thousands of journal templates.

name: Narrow One
tagline: Multiplayer bow-and-arrow battles in your browser.
description: Narrow One is a free multiplayer archery game — capture the flag with friends in quick browser matches, no install or account required.

name: Gartic Phone
tagline: The telephone game with drawings — chaos guaranteed.
description: Gartic Phone is the hugely popular party game where players alternate writing prompts and drawing them, producing gloriously garbled results to review together.

name: skribbl.io
tagline: Free multiplayer drawing and guessing game.
description: skribbl.io is the classic browser Pictionary — one player draws, everyone else races to guess the word. Private rooms make it a video-call favorite.

name: RetroArch Web
tagline: The RetroArch emulator frontend running in your browser.
description: The official web build of RetroArch runs classic game system emulator cores directly in the browser. Bring your own legally obtained game files.

name: Wordle
tagline: The daily five-letter word puzzle that took over the world.
description: Wordle gives you six tries to guess the day's five-letter word, with color-coded hints after each guess. Published daily by The New York Times.

name: Backgammon Galaxy
tagline: Play backgammon online with instant match analysis.
description: Backgammon Galaxy pairs you with players worldwide and analyzes every move against the computer's best play, making it a favorite for improving players.

name: GeoGuessr
tagline: Get dropped anywhere on Earth — guess where you are.
description: GeoGuessr drops you into a random street view and challenges you to pin the location on a map — the closer your guess, the more points you score.

name: Hacker Typer
tagline: Mash the keyboard, look like a movie hacker.
description: Hacker Typer fills the screen with convincing-looking code no matter what you type — the internet's favorite way to pretend you're in a heist film.

name: FastTrack
tagline: Track intermittent fasting windows offline.
description: FastTrack is a small open-source intermittent fasting timer — start a fast, watch the window, and review history, all stored on your device.

name: Cronometer
tagline: Track nutrition down to 84 micronutrients.
description: Cronometer is the nutrition tracker known for data accuracy — log food against lab-verified databases and see vitamins and minerals, not just calories.

name: Inoreader
tagline: A powerful RSS reader for serious news followers.
description: Inoreader is a full-featured feed reader — follow websites, newsletters, and social feeds in one inbox with rules, filters, and offline reading.

name: Hacker News PWA
tagline: Hacker News readers built as reference PWAs.
description: HNPWA collects Hacker News reader implementations built with different frameworks as reference Progressive Web Apps — pick one and browse HN fast.

name: Readwise Reader
tagline: Save articles, PDFs, and newsletters — read and highlight anywhere.
description: Reader by Readwise is a read-it-later app that unifies articles, PDFs, ebooks, and newsletters with excellent highlighting that syncs to your notes tools.

name: OpenStreetMap
tagline: The free, editable map of the whole world.
description: OpenStreetMap is the Wikipedia of maps — a world map built by millions of contributors, free to use and powering thousands of other apps.

name: Flightradar24
tagline: Watch every flight in the sky in real time.
description: Flightradar24 tracks aircraft worldwide live on a map — tap any plane for route, altitude, speed, and aircraft details. The standard for flight tracking.

name: Felt
tagline: Make and share beautiful custom maps in minutes.
description: Felt is a modern collaborative mapping tool — draw on maps, add data layers, and share interactive maps as easily as a document.

name: Bitwarden
tagline: The trusted open-source password manager's web vault.
description: Bitwarden's web vault gives you full access to your encrypted passwords from any browser. Open source, independently audited, with a generous free tier.

name: QRCode Monkey
tagline: Free QR codes with logos, colors, and high resolution.
description: QRCode Monkey generates free QR codes with custom colors, shapes, and embedded logos, exportable in print-quality resolution with no account.

name: WordCounter
tagline: Count words and characters, check reading level.
description: WordCounter counts words, characters, sentences, and paragraphs as you type, with reading-time and keyword-density extras for writers and students.

name: Notella
tagline: Ultra-quick notes that stay on your device.
description: A tiny note-taking app for jotting things down instantly. Listed from a community directory; we could not fully verify the publisher.

name: Morse Code Translator
tagline: Translate to and from Morse code with sound and light.
description: Morse Code World's translator converts text to Morse and back with authentic audio playback, flashing light, and adjustable speed — great for learning.

name: Calculator.net
tagline: Hundreds of free calculators for every situation.
description: Calculator.net hosts hundreds of specialized calculators — mortgages, BMI, percentages, dates, loans — each simple, fast, and free.

name: TableConvert
tagline: Convert tables between Excel, Markdown, JSON, and more.
description: TableConvert transforms table data between formats — paste from Excel and get Markdown, LaTeX, SQL, JSON, or CSV out, with an editable grid in between.

name: TinyWow
tagline: Free PDF, image, and video mini-tools — no signup.
description: TinyWow offers hundreds of small free tools for PDFs, images, video, and writing with no account or watermarks. Supported by ads.

name: Padloc
tagline: A clean, open-source password manager for everyone.
description: Padloc is an open-source password manager focused on simplicity — end-to-end encrypted vaults with apps for every platform and a clear, friendly interface.

name: PrivateBin
tagline: Encrypted pastebin — the server can't read your paste.
description: PrivateBin is the widely deployed open-source pastebin where pastes are encrypted in the browser before upload, with optional expiry and burn-after-reading.

name: GIPHY GIF Maker
tagline: Turn videos and clips into GIFs with captions.
description: GIPHY's official GIF Maker turns video files, YouTube links, or images into GIFs with captions and stickers, ready to share anywhere.

name: ShareDrop
tagline: AirDrop-style file sharing between any devices.
description: ShareDrop transfers files directly between devices on the same network (or via link) using peer-to-peer connections — nothing is stored on a server.

name: 2brew
tagline: Step-by-step timers for brewing better coffee.
description: 2brew guides you through coffee brewing recipes — AeroPress, V60, French press — with step-by-step timers. Listed from a community directory.

name: Unsplash
tagline: Over five million freely usable high-quality photos.
description: Unsplash is the internet's source of freely usable images — millions of high-resolution photos gifted by photographers, searchable and free for most uses.

name: Polarr Photo Editor
tagline: Pro-grade photo editing with filters and masks.
description: Polarr's web photo editor brings advanced editing — curves, selective masks, and stylish filters — to the browser, with offline support once loaded.

name: Google Keep
tagline: Quick notes, lists, and reminders synced to your Google account.
description: Google Keep captures notes, checklists, and reminders and syncs them instantly across all your devices. Sign in with a Google account, then install it as an app straight from the browser.

name: kromans-Reader
tagline: Photograph pages of a physical book and have them read aloud in order.
description: Reader turns printed pages into audio: photograph the pages of a physical book and the app reads them back to you in order. Books are stored on your device, it works offline once installed, and no account is needed. Personally built and verified by the Web App Finder curator.

name: OpenRouter
tagline: Chat with hundreds of AI models through one account.
description: OpenRouter is a marketplace and router for AI models: one account gives you chat and API access to hundreds of models from many providers, with unified billing and automatic fallbacks. The installed app opens straight into its chat interface.

name: kromans-Sports
tagline: Live scores, finals, and results for the teams and golfers you follow.
description: kromans-Sports tracks your teams across NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, college sports, golf, and international soccer including the World Cup. Follow up to five teams free, see live scores while the app is open, browse championship and playoff games in every sport, and tap through to each team's full season results. Installs to your home screen and works without an account.

name: kromans-Wanted
tagline: Photograph incoming secondhand stock and instantly match it to the customers who asked for it.
description: Built for the shop counter: staff photograph an arriving item, AI identifies it, and the app checks it against the list of open customer requests — showing who asked for it, when, and a one-tap email to tell them it came in. Typed and spoken descriptions work too, requests expire on a schedule you set, and both requests and scans keep printable logs. Personally built and verified by the Web App Finder curator.

name: kromans-DocsTranslate
tagline: Translate a page of your document — pick your quality, see the cost first.
description: DocsTranslate translates one page at a time (pasted text or a single-page PDF) at three quality levels, always quoting the price before you commit. Your first 9 pages are free — 3 at each quality level — so you can prove it works on your phone before buying a $9.95 Translation Pack, and the app checks your device is up to the job before it lets you pay. Installs to your home screen and needs no account. Personally built and verified by the Web App Finder curator.
